meta.title: Salesforce CRM Interview Questions | Teks Academy
meta.description: Explore top Salesforce CRM interview questions and answers covering Apex, Lightning Web Components, Salesforce Administration, Automation, and CRM workflows.

heading: Salesforce CRM
Interview Questions
heroSection.subHeading: Prepare for Salesforce CRM interviews with top administrator and developer interview questions and answers.

questions.question: What is Salesforce?
questions.answer: Salesforce is a cloud-based Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform that helps businesses manage sales, service, marketing, and more.

questions.question: What is an object in Salesforce?
questions.answer: An object is a database table that allows you to store data specific to your organization in Salesforce.

questions.question: What are the types of objects in Salesforce?
questions.answer: There are two types: Standard Objects (provided by Salesforce) and Custom Objects (created by users).

questions.question: What is a custom object?
questions.answer: A custom object is a user-defined object that stores data unique to your organization.

questions.question: What is a standard object?
questions.answer: Standard objects are pre-built objects provided by Salesforce, such as Accounts, Contacts, Leads, and Opportunities.

questions.question: What is a field in Salesforce?
questions.answer: A field is a single piece of data attached to an object, similar to a column in a database.

questions.question: What is a record in Salesforce?
questions.answer: A record is a row of data in an object; each record is an instance of an object.

questions.question: What is a relationship in Salesforce?
questions.answer: Relationships are used to link objects together. Common types are Lookup and Master-Detail relationships.

questions.question: What is a Lookup relationship?
questions.answer: A Lookup relationship links two objects loosely, allowing one to reference another.

questions.question: What is a Master-Detail relationship?
questions.answer: A Master-Detail relationship creates a strong dependency between objects; the detail record's lifecycle depends on the master.

questions.question: What is a trigger in Salesforce?
questions.answer: A trigger is Apex code that executes before or after specific data manipulation language (DML) events on a particular object.

questions.question: What is Apex?
questions.answer: Apex is a strongly typed, object-oriented programming language used by developers to execute flow and transaction control statements on Salesforce servers.

questions.question: What is a workflow rule?
questions.answer: Workflow rules automate standard internal procedures to save time across your org.

questions.question: What is a validation rule?
questions.answer: Validation rules verify that the data entered by users meets certain criteria before the record can be saved.

questions.question: What is a formula field?
questions.answer: A formula field is a read-only field that derives its value from a formula expression defined by the user.

questions.question: What is a roll-up summary field?
questions.answer: A roll-up summary field calculates values from related records, such as the sum of related opportunities on an account.

questions.question: What is a profile in Salesforce?
questions.answer: A profile controls object permissions, field-level access, user permissions, and more.

questions.question: What is a role in Salesforce?
questions.answer: Roles control the level of visibility that users have to organization data.

questions.question: What is permission set?
questions.answer: Permission sets grant additional permissions to users without changing their profile.

questions.question: What is the difference between profile and permission set?
questions.answer: Profiles define baseline access; permission sets add extra permissions without altering the profile.

questions.question: What is a sandbox?
questions.answer: A sandbox is a copy of the production environment used for development, testing, and training.

questions.question: What are the types of sandboxes?
questions.answer: Developer, Developer Pro, Partial Copy, and Full Sandbox.

questions.question: What is SOQL?
questions.answer: SOQL (Salesforce Object Query Language) is used to query data from Salesforce objects.

questions.question: What is SOSL?
questions.answer: SOSL (Salesforce Object Search Language) is used to perform text searches in records.

questions.question: What is the difference between SOQL and SOSL?
questions.answer: SOQL retrieves records from a single object; SOSL searches text across multiple objects.

questions.question: What is a report in Salesforce?
questions.answer: A report is a list of records that meet the criteria you define.

questions.question: What is a dashboard?
questions.answer: A dashboard is a visual display of key metrics and trends for records in your org.

questions.question: What is a Lightning Component?
questions.answer: A Lightning Component is a UI framework for developing dynamic web apps for mobile and desktop devices.

questions.question: What is the difference between Classic and Lightning?
questions.answer: Lightning offers a modern UI and additional features not available in Classic.

questions.question: What is the use of AppExchange?
questions.answer: AppExchange is Salesforce's marketplace for apps, components, and consulting services.

questions.question: What is Governor Limit?
questions.answer: Governor Limits are runtime limits enforced by Salesforce to ensure efficient use of resources.

questions.question: What is a static resource?
questions.answer: Static resources allow you to upload files like images, JavaScript, and CSS to reference in Visualforce or Lightning components.

questions.question: What is an Apex class?
questions.answer: An Apex class is a template or blueprint from which Apex objects are created.

questions.question: What is a test class in Salesforce?
questions.answer: Test classes ensure your code works as expected and help meet the code coverage requirement for deployment.

questions.question: What is the use of batch Apex?
questions.answer: Batch Apex is used to process large data volumes asynchronously in batches.

questions.question: What is a future method?
questions.answer: Future methods are used to perform long-running operations asynchronously.

questions.question: What is a queueable Apex?
questions.answer: Queueable Apex allows you to add jobs to a queue and is more flexible than future methods.

questions.question: What is a wrapper class?
questions.answer: A wrapper class is a custom object defined by a developer to group multiple fields or objects.

questions.question: What is the use of Chatter?
questions.answer: Chatter is a Salesforce collaboration tool that lets users work together, talk to each other, and share information securely.

questions.question: What is deployment in Salesforce?
questions.answer: Deployment is the process of moving metadata from one Salesforce organization to another.

questions.question: What tools are used for deployment?
questions.answer: Change Sets, ANT Migration Tool, and Salesforce DX.

questions.question: What is schema builder?
questions.answer: Schema Builder is a tool that lets you visualize and edit your data model.

questions.question: What is a junction object?
questions.answer: A junction object is a custom object with two master-detail relationships, used to create many-to-many relationships.

questions.question: What is data loader?
questions.answer: Data Loader is a client application to bulk import, export, update, or delete data.

questions.question: What is the use of the recycle bin?
questions.answer: The recycle bin allows you to restore accidentally deleted records within a certain period.

questions.question: What is a record type?
questions.answer: Record types allow you to offer different business processes, picklist values, and page layouts to different users.

questions.question: What is a page layout?
questions.answer: Page layouts control the layout and organization of fields, buttons, and related lists on object record pages.

questions.question: What is a flow?
questions.answer: Flow is an automation tool that collects data and performs actions in Salesforce without code.

questions.question: What is a Lightning Web Component (LWC)?
questions.answer: LWC is a new programming model built on web standards for building fast, reusable components.

questions.question: What is Salesforce DX?
questions.answer: Salesforce DX is a set of tools and features that improve the end-to-end development lifecycle and enable continuous delivery on the Salesforce platform.
